rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required in light of the declaration filed by the beneficiary's attorney on 07/31/2023 purporting to request payment of fees and costs from the trust. The trustee has not amended her petition to include those fees and costs as additional relief sought (if the trustee supports the request), nor has the beneficiary filed his own petition to compel the trustee to pay his attorney's fees and costs. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Statement Of Interest And Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interests In Estate And Authorizing Distribution Directly To Celtic Research
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/02/2023, as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 06/12/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Direct notice must be given to the personal representative. See CRC 7.51(a). 4) Direct notice must be given to Bridge Financial Funding LLC as a person who requested special notice, in addition to a separate copy given to its counsel. 5) Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must be served on all persons who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. 6) The petition must be signed by an authorized representative of Celtic Research. See CRC 7.103(a). 7) Is the relief sought by this petition premature, given that no accountings have been filed? See Estate of Ramsey (1951) 107 Cal.App.2d 372, 375 [Clearly it is within the discretion of the court to conduct such hearings in advance as well as at the time of the hearing of a petition for distribution. However, in the present case, the hearing was had prior to the filing of any account by the executor, although it was necessary for the court to know the value of the interests of appellants in the residuary estate in order to determine whether the sum they had received for their assignments was fair or grossly inadequate. We think that where interests have been assigned outright the court might properly refuse to conduct a hearing under section 1020.1 in advance of a hearing upon an account of the executor or administrator.]; see also Estate of Boyd (1979) 98 Cal.App.3d 125, 134 ["The import of the holding in Ramsey is that the court must have some reliable foundation on which to base its evaluation of the property. Where no executor's account had been filed, the court "might properly refuse to conduct a hearing under section 1020.1."] An appearance may be required on 10/02/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND BESSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Proof of service of notice shows that notice was mailed on 7/23/23, which was a Sunday, so effective notice did not begin to toll until Monday, 7/24/23, when any mail would have been processed, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) No proof of publication has been filed. (5) No Receipt and Acknowledgement of Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (6) It appears that the petitioner was named in the lost will as the executor, so the petition caption appears to be incorrect. (7) Items 2a, 2b, and 2d of the petition must be completed. (8) Items 3a, 3c, 3d, and 3e of the petition must be completed. (9) The will file has only a photocopy of the will, so item 3f(3) of the petition should be checked, and an attachment 3f(3) must be attached, stating reasons why the petitioner asserts the will has not been revoked. (Evidence will be required to overcome the presumption of revocation. Probate Code Section 6124.) (10) Item 3h of the petition is incomplete (11) Items 5a(3) or (4) and (7) or (8) must be answered. (12) Item 7b(3) of the petition should not be answered when issue survived. (13) The relationships to the decedent of the persons listed in item 8 of the petition are missing. (14) It is unclear why a "Response Examiner Notes Dated May 16, 2022 for Petition for Probate" filed on 7/6/22 was attached to this new petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 10/5/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CLARENCE JOSEPH SUGHROUE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although there was no reconciliation shown between the ending balance of cash in the savings account shown in the accounting and the amount shown on the statement ending 10/5/22, and the petition caption did not disclose the request for a lien (and, in any event, ordered fees may be filed as a creditor's claim in the estate of the deceased conservatee). The Court makes no finding of the reasonableness of fees requested; they are granted solely at the request of the conservator who is the sole beneficiary of the conservatee's estate. (Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition), not in response to Examiner's Notes, which are not filed or otherwise part of the record.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMMA CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/28/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/21/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) The supplemental declaration filed on 04/12/2023 is treated as an amendment to the petition requiring new notice to be given at least 15 days before a continued hearing. CRC 7.3(4) & 7.53(a). Note: Item 1 of the DE-120 form must specifically list the supplemental declaration as an amendment to the petition. 3) An allegation must be made as to whether there are outstanding bills due to a third party for preparing this account. LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a)(iii). 4) An allegation must be made as to whether the estate cash has been kept in interest-bearing accounts. Probate Code section 1064(a)(5). 5) A proof must be filed to show that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 9730. 6) The filing fee for the spousal property petition was $435. 7) An original small estate affidavit signed by Denise Chew, together with a certified copy of Jane Leong's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 8) An original small estate affidavit signed by Robert Yee, Eric Yee, and Debra Yee, together with a certified copy of Cobina Wyatt's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 9) An original small estate affidavit signed by Valerie Smith, Sharon Chan, and Jeffrey Chan, together with a certified copy of Rosa Chan's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 10) According to the second supplement filed on 09/16/2021, Cobina Wyatt was survived by her spouse. The issue of how distribution to and from Cobina Wyatt's postdeceased spouse can be made must be addressed. See section 11802. 11) According to the second supplement filed on 09/16/2021, Rosa Chan was survived by her spouse. The issue of how distribution to and from Rosa Chan's postdeceased spouse can be made must be addressed. See section 11802.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/25/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/14/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) Please identify all heirs of the settlor with a family tree including names and dates of death of all deceased relatives who would have been heirs. 2) If there is no personal representative of the settlor's probate estate, each heir of the settlor must be served in the manner required by Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(b) & CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 3) Re the DE-115 form: i) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 30 days before the hearing. ii) Item 3 must list the correct last 4 digits of the account number. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY SUEY HAI YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need information if Lotus Yee has any legal representation (for example, conservator or attorney-in-fact) who also should have received the notice on behalf of her. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PRISCILLA D. LUMBA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/30/2023 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 07/25/2023).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IDA FLEISHER REVOCABLE TRUST OF JUNE 28, 2006 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Cofirming Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. (Although proof of service shows that the incorrect form of Notice of Hearing was served (DE-120 instead of DE-115), since the successor trustee is the sole heir and only remainder beneficiary, notice was not required in any event. A copy of the current vesting deed was also not attached to the petition, only a copy of an un-recorded Grant Deed, which was not actually proof that the deceased settlor owned a 100% interest in the real property as her sole and separate property.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DANNY SIU-CHAN YAN AND BOONRAT A. YAN REVOCABLE TRUST dated February 12, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Removing Trustee, Appointing Interim Trustee Pending Trial, And Appointing Successor; For Surcharge And Other Relief; For Double
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/25/2023 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 08/01/2023). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-115 form. 3) Item 1 of the DE-115 form must state the complete title of the petition. CRC 7.50. 4) The petition must be amended to include relief under Probate Code section 850 in the caption. See CRC 7.102. 5) The trustee must be served in the manner required by section 851(a)(1) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. as to the request for relief under section 850. 6) The address of the trustee's attorney alleged in the petition and listed on the proof of service attached to the DE-115 form appears incorrect.
